blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Infections of the Skin?
Skin infections ใน neonate = อันตรายกว่าที่คิด เพราะ skin barrier + immunity + renal function ยัง immature โดยเฉพาะ preterm. 6 ข้อสำคัญ: (1) S. aureus = most versatile pathogen — invasive incidence 44.8/10,000 NICUs (Ericson JAMA Pediatr 2015); ทำได้ทั้ง bullous impetigo, SSSS, omphalitis, abscess, sepsis; (2) SSSS ≠ TEN: แยกด้วย biopsy — SSSS = intraepidermal cleavage (ไม่มีรอยแผลเป็น); TEN = full-thickness necrosis + mucosal involvement + drug-induced; (3) Invasive Candidiasis mortality 20–30% ใน VLBW; echinocandins ห้ามใช้ถ้า eye involvement; (4) fluconazole prophylaxis ใช้เฉพาะ NICU ที่มี IC ≥15% เท่านั้น (Benjamin JAMA 2014 RCT); (5) HSV SEM: acyclovir 60 mg/kg/day IV × 14 วัน — อย่า delay — ถ้า untreated: ¾ progress to CNS/disseminated; (6) 80% ของมารดาที่ถ่าย HSV ให้ลูกไม่มี known history ของ genital herpes → index of suspicion สูงเสมอ.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย mechanism ของ bullous impetigo vs SSSS: exfoliative toxin A vs B, desmoglein 1 cleavage, local vs circulating toxin
แยก SSSS จาก TEN ด้วย clinical features + frozen section biopsy; รู้ว่า mucosal involvement อยู่ใน TEN แต่ไม่อยู่ใน SSSS
รู้ antibiotic ที่ใช้ใน SSSS: oxacillin ± clindamycin; indication ของ MRSA coverage; ทำไม clindamycin เป็น first-line option
วินิจฉัยและจัดการ omphalitis: organisms, risk factors, empiric therapy (ampicillin + gentamicin), cord care ต่างกันระหว่าง hospital vs community
แยก oral thrush vs Candida diaper dermatitis vs congenital candidiasis (CCC) vs invasive candidiasis (IC): presentation, diagnosis, treatment
รู้ risk factors ของ IC ใน VLBW; รู้ว่า fluconazole prophylaxis ใช้เมื่อใด; รู้ข้อห้าม echinocandin ใน ophthalmologic involvement
จำแนก neonatal HSV: SEM (45%) vs CNS (30%) vs Disseminated (25%); รู้ prognosis + mortality ของแต่ละ type
รู้การ diagnose HSV: PCR preferred, Tzanck unreliable; ส่ง specimens จากที่ไหนบ้าง; PCR sensitivity ใน CSF 75–100%
รู้ treatment HSV: acyclovir 60 mg/kg/day (20 mg/kg/dose q8h); SEM 14 days; CNS/disseminated 21 days; repeat CSF PCR ถ้า CNS
รู้ DDx vesicopustular eruptions ใน neonate (Box 93.1) ครบทั้ง 8 รายการ
1. Staphylococcus aureus — Impetigo
1.1 Epidemiology & Colonization
S. aureus = commensal ใน ~1/3 population; ชอบอยู่ที่ nares, perineum, moist surfaces
C-section delivery → colonized via maternal adult skin microbiota (ไม่ใช่ vaginal flora) → S. aureus พบเร็วกว่า vaginal birth
By 1 week ของชีวิต: ส่วนใหญ่ colonized ที่ nasopharynx หรือ umbilicus
Invasive S. aureus in NICU: 44.8/10,000 infants (retrospective 348 NICUs — Ericson JAMA Pediatr 2015); most MSSA (methicillin-sensitive strains)
Toxin-mediated lesions: exfoliative toxins A (ETA) and B (ETB) → cleave desmoglein 1 → diverse skin manifestations (protean morphology)
1.2 Bullous Impetigo — Clinical Features
Lesions: flaccid vesicles/bullae หรือ pustules on erythematous base — most common ที่ diaper area, periumbilical, skin folds
Ruptured → moist red base + collarette of scale (characteristic); heals without scarring
Lesions ไม่ closely grouped → แยกจาก HSV infection (ซึ่ง grouped vesicles)
Aggressive: widespread desquamation in degloving pattern (Nguyen et al. Pediatr Dermatol 2016)
1.3 Nonbullous Impetigo
Thin-walled vesicles/pustules at sites of broken skin — extremities, face
Rupture → honey-colored crusts on eroded red patches; variably pruritic
Causative organism: S. aureus (primary) + GAS (group A streptococcus) may co-infect nonbullous form
1.4 Pathogenesis — Exfoliative Toxins
Bullous impetigo: ETA or ETB produced locally → cleave desmoglein 1 (desmosomal protein) ใน superficial epidermis → loss of keratinocyte adhesion → flaccid blisters
ETA > ETB ใน bullous impetigo; ETB > ETA ใน SSSS
1.5 Treatment of Bullous Impetigo
Limited infection: topical mupirocin — ใช้ด้วยความระมัดระวังใน neonates
More extensive: systemic penicillinase-resistant antibiotic × 7–10 days (IV preferred); ต้อง guide by cultures
MRSA coverage: clindamycin, vancomycin หรือ linezolid
Periumbilical lesions → risk of omphalitis — monitor closely
Isolation จนกว่า lesions หยุด spread + no crusting + reepithelializing
2. Staphylococcal Scalded Skin Syndrome (SSSS)
2.1 Pathogenesis — Local vs Circulating Toxin
Bullous impetigo = local ETA/ETB effect; SSSS = circulating ETB (mainly) from distant S. aureus focus
ทำไม neonates susceptible: immunologic immaturity + renal immaturity → ↓ anti-exfoliative toxin antibodies + impaired toxin clearance → accumulate in circulation
Primary S. aureus focus: nose, mouth, conjunctiva, umbilicus, circumcision site; blister fluid = sterile (S. aureus ไม่อยู่ที่ skin lesion)
2.2 Clinical Findings
Abrupt onset: temperature instability + lethargy + irritability + generalized skin tenderness + erythema
Initial erythema: face → spreads to intertriginous areas; facial swelling + conjunctivitis + periorificial crusting with radial fissuring around mouth
Mucosal surfaces SPARED in SSSS ← key DDx from TEN
Progression: flaccid bullae → desquamation within hours to days; Nikolsky sign positive (light stroking → skin separation)
Denuded areas: painful, shiny, ↑ risk thermoregulation + fluid/electrolyte imbalance + superinfection (gram-negative organisms)
2.3 SSSS vs TEN — Key Distinctions
2.4 Diagnosis of SSSS
Frozen section biopsy: intraepidermal blister = SSSS; dermal-epidermal split = TEN — ช่วย initiate treatment ได้เร็ว
Swabs from potential foci (nasopharynx, conjunctiva, umbilicus) → Gram stain emergently + culture
Blood culture: sepsis อาจเกิดได้ แม้ uncommon
Bullous lesions: do NOT contain organisms (blister fluid sterile)
2.5 Treatment of SSSS
Empiric: cephalosporin หรือ oxacillin (penicillinase-resistant penicillin) as first choice
Consider adding MRSA coverage (vancomycin/clindamycin) ถ้า high MRSA prevalence area หรือ severe systemic disease
Clindamycin advantage: superior cutaneous penetration + inhibits toxin production — Wang et al. 2020: antibiotic sensitivity varies by geography; some areas clindamycin-resistant strains predominate
Empiric combo: oxacillin + clindamycin จนกว่าจะได้ sensitivities (Braunstein et al. Pediatr Dermatol 2014)
Widespread disease: fluid + nutrition support + thermoregulation; normal saline compresses + bland ointment emollient ใน desquamating areas
After therapy 2–3 days: areas dry + desquamate; heals without scarring (superficial intraepidermal plane)
2.6 Nursery Outbreaks
S. aureus outbreaks ใน nursery: may be underreported; present as multiple SSSS, pustulosis, bullous impetigo, abscesses
Risk factors: crowded nurseries, poor hand hygiene, inadequate umbilical cord care, staff carriage, lack of early isolation

Prevention: universal hand-washing protocols, ↓ overcrowding, ↑ nurse:patient ratio, staff monitoring for carriage; active surveillance cultures + mupirocin decolonization + chlorhexidine baths (Popoola et al. Infect Control Hosp Epidemiol 2016)
3. Streptococcal Infections & Omphalitis
3.1 Group A Streptococcus (GAS)
GAS infections ใน nursery: less common กว่า staph แต่ documented outbreaks มีอยู่; introduced via maternal carriers/nursery staff
Manifestations: omphalitis, cellulitis, pustular eruptions, paronychia; dissemination → respiratory distress, toxic shock-like syndrome, lethargy, abdominal distension
Note: fever อาจ absent ใน neonates <5 days; meningitis มักพบใน infants ≥5 days
Treatment: penicillin (GAS responds readily); penicillin prophylaxis + umbilical disinfection เพื่อป้องกัน nursery spread
3.2 Group B Streptococcus (GBS)
GBS = primary cause of neonatal sepsis; one of most frequently encountered pathogens in nursery
Early-onset (<7 days): septicemia + respiratory distress + shock (acquired during labor/delivery)
Late-onset (7 days – 3 months): post-birth acquisition; meningitis + adverse neurodevelopmental outcomes more common
Skin manifestations uncommon; most common = cellulitis ของ face/neck (GBS cellulitis-adenitis syndrome)
GBS cellulitis-adenitis: age 1–11 weeks; progressive erythema + swelling; fever + irritability + poor oral intake; blood culture often positive; CSF cultures recommended
Also: erosions, abscesses, necrotizing fasciitis
Treatment: penicillin G or ampicillin first-line
3.3 Omphalitis
Definition: bacterial infection ของ umbilical stump; presents ~day 3 of life
Organisms: S. aureus, S. epidermidis, Streptococcus spp., E. coli, Clostridium difficile, Klebsiella, Pseudomonas; contracted from birth canal/nonsterile exposures
Risk factors: unplanned home birth, septic delivery, prematurity, low birth weight, umbilical cord catheterization, prolonged ROM, perinatal maternal infection (Fraser et al. Acta Paediatr 2006)

Clinical: edema + erythema + tenderness around umbilicus; purulent malodorous discharge; may extend → cellulitis, lymphangitis, necrotizing fasciitis, sepsis, peritonitis, bowel ischemia, abscesses, hepatic vein thrombosis
Diagnosis: Gram stain + culture from moist umbilical stump fluid (note: easily contaminated — clinical correlation needed); แยกจาก embryonic duct remnants
Treatment: empiric ampicillin + gentamicin IV (covers gram-positive + gram-negative); switch to oral antibiotic เมื่อ skin improves

rows: Dry cord care — keep clean, open to air or covered with clean cloth
Home birth / low-resource / high neonatal mortality

blocks.text: 4. Candida Species Infections — Localized
4.1 General Pathogenesis
C. albicans = commensal of gut, respiratory + cutaneous surfaces; colonization ผ่าน maternal genitourinary tract เป็นหลัก
Factors promoting infection: ↓ host immunity, altered surface microbiota, epidermal immaturity; infection may develop in utero, during delivery, or postnatally
4.2 Oral Candidiasis (Thrush)
Adherent white patches on buccal mucosa/tongue on normal or erythematous base; recalcitrant to physical removal
Peak C. albicans colonization of oropharynx: ~4 weeks age; 13.6% incidence in neonates <1 month (Turkish outpatient study — Yilmaz et al. 2011)
More common ถ้า maternal symptomatic vulvovaginitis (vs simply colonized)
Mode of delivery ไม่ associate กับ risk of oral thrush (St. Clair-Brown et al. J Am Board Fam Med 2018)
Treatment: nystatin suspension 100,000–200,000 units QID × 7–14 days; refractory → ↑ dose nystatin หรือ systemic fluconazole; breastfeeding mother → treat mother also
Gentian (crystal) violet 0.5–1% aqueous: effective but purple staining + mucosal ulceration + carcinogenicity (mice) — less commonly used
4.3 Candida Diaper Dermatitis
Widespread confluent erythema + scalloped edges + intertriginous involvement; peripheral white scale + satellite papules/pustules (hallmark)
Also infects neck folds, axillae, nails
Diagnosis: KOH prep → budding yeasts + pseudohyphae; confirm by culture
Treatment: topical antifungals — nystatin, imidazole (miconazole, ketoconazole), ciclopirox olamine
Miconazole 0.25% ointment × 7 days: ~50% clinical cure rate for diaper candidiasis; recurrent episodes higher cure; ไม่ทำให้เกิด resistance (Blanco & van Rossem Pediatr Dermatol 2013)
5. Congenital & Invasive Candidiasis
5.1 Congenital Cutaneous Candidiasis (CCC)
Ascending intrauterine infection: Candida spp. ข้าม fetal membrane → infects surfaces in contact with amniotic fluid
Timing: present within 6 days of birth; most commonly day 1 of life
Classic lesions: erythematous macules, papules, vesicopustules + intense background erythema; back + extensor extremities + intertriginous areas
Distinguishing features vs other dermatoses:
Palmar + plantar pustules — uncommon in other benign neonatal rashes
Paronychia + nail involvement (onychomadesis + yellow hyperkeratotic bands)
Face, oral mucosa, diaper area comparatively spared (opposite of candida diaper rash)
In VLBW (<1000g): scald-like erythema; prognosis guarded; CCC may present with burn-like dermatitis
Full-term uncomplicated: skin lesions resolve with desquamation within 1–2 weeks; nail changes improve spontaneously
Diagnosis: KOH prep → budding yeasts + pseudohyphae; cultures usually negative but must be sent in all preterm infants + when systemic disease suspected
5.2 Treatment of CCC
Well full-term infant: active nonintervention + close monitoring (spontaneous resolution 1–2 wk); topical antifungals อาจใช้
At-risk neonates (VLBW/preterm): empiric systemic treatment ทันที while awaiting cultures
Systemic treatment: amphotericin B or fluconazole ≥14 days — shown to prevent dissemination + mortality (Kaufman et al. Clin Infect Dis 2017)
Additional risk factors for systemic treatment: antibiotics/steroids, indwelling catheters, parenteral nutrition, respiratory distress, burn-like dermatitis
5.3 Disseminated / Invasive Candidiasis (IC)
Definition: Candida in otherwise sterile body fluid (blood, urine, CSF); typically acquired postnatally; presents after first week of life (later than CCC)
Incidence: uncommon ใน >2500g; 5–10% ใน <1000g; organisms: C. albicans + C. parapsilosis
Mortality: ~20–30% across all affected neonates; high morbidity in survivors
Risk Factors for IC

rows: Parenteral nutrition with IV lipid emulsions

blocks.text: Clinical Findings of IC
Skin: erosive burn-like dermatitis → desquamation, scaly/erythematous patches, papules, pustules, abscesses; intertriginous accentuation + oral mucosal involvement
Systemic spread (hematogenous from GI + cutaneous reservoirs): kidney, CNS, eyes (most common); also spleen, liver, heart, bones, joints
Labs: ↑ WBC, thrombocytopenia, persistent hyperglycemia + glycosuria
Systemic signs: respiratory distress, temperature instability, apnea, bradycardia, abdominal distension, hypotension
Diagnosis of IC
Blood/CSF culture: sensitivity ≤50% — limited; urine culture often false-positive but strongly associated with systemic disease ใน at-risk infants
Touch prep from punch biopsy: imprint dermal side on slide → KOH or Gram stain → fast diagnosis
Workup: blood + urine + CSF cultures + ophthalmologic exam + echo + CXR + head/abdominal US
Treatment of IC
First-line: fluconazole หรือ amphotericin B (liposomal amphotericin B ถ้า urinary tract NOT involved)
Echinocandins (caspofungin): ไม่เหมาะถ้า ophthalmologic involvement — cannot penetrate vitreous
Duration: 21 days after microbiologic clearance (confirmed by culture + imaging)

Fluconazole prophylaxis: ใช้ได้ใน <1000g ที่อยู่ใน NICU ที่มี IC rate ≥15% — แต่ Benjamin JAMA 2014 RCT ใน NICUs with lower rates (US/EU): ไม่พบการลด IC หรือ death → universal prophylaxis ไม่เหมาะสำหรับ NICUs ที่มี IC rate ต่ำ
Sequelae of IC: neurodevelopmental impairment, deafness, retinopathy of prematurity, CLD, renal failure
6. Aspergillosis & Tinea in Neonates
6.1 Primary Cutaneous Aspergillosis (PCA)
Increasingly common in extremely premature; ↑ incidence due to improved survival of ELBW + hospital construction → airborne fungal spores
Route: traumatic inoculation via IV line placement, adhesive tape, elastic dressings, armboards, cyanoacrylate adhesives, contaminated gauze (Kusari et al. Pediatr Dermatol 2018)
Contributing factors: immature skin barrier + immune system, systemic steroids, broad-spectrum antibiotics
Lesions: erythematous papule/patch/plaque → pustular → cutaneous ulceration with crusting ± purpura
DDx: other deep fungal infections, cutaneous Pseudomonas, polyarteritis nodosa, pyoderma gangrenosum
Diagnosis: skin biopsy (histology + tissue culture); exclude systemic involvement (blood culture, CXR, echo, funduscopic, LP, abdominal US)
Treatment: amphotericin B (liposomal formulation preferred); voriconazole as alternative; single lesions → surgical debridement to prevent dissemination
6.2 Tinea Capitis & Tinea Corporis
Uncommon in neonates แต่ important เพราะ mistaken for more serious disorders; caused by Trichophyton + Microsporum spp.
Risk factors in NICU: moist/humid environment, broad-spectrum antibiotics, frequent tapes/instrumentation
Tinea capitis: alopecia + erythema + scale ± lymphadenopathy; may be annular
Tinea corporis: annular patch with central clearing + peripheral erythema/scale; atypical: pustules, nodules, vesicles
DDx: neonatal lupus, Langerhans cell histiocytosis, impetigo, HSV, seborrheic dermatitis
Diagnosis: KOH prep (bedside); dermatophyte culture; skin biopsy (atypical)
Treatment: topical azole antifungal หรือ terbinafine BID–TID — effective even for scalp infections ใน neonates; low systemic absorption
7. Herpes Simplex Virus (HSV) — Classification & Clinical
7.1 Epidemiology & Transmission
Incidence: 1.6–33/100,000 live births ใน developed countries
Most neonatal HSV ใน developed countries: HSV-1 (↑ incidence of HSV-1 genital herpes)
Transmission: 85% peripartum (intrapartum contact with genital mucosa during active/asymptomatic shedding); ~5% in utero; ~10% postnatal
Risk ↑ with: primary maternal infection >> reactivation; first episode nonprimary; vaginal delivery; prolonged ROM; fetal scalp electrodes; HSV-1 maternal infection
80% ของ transmitting mothers have NO known history of genital herpes (James & Kimberlin Infect Dis Clin North Am 2015)
7.2 Classification of Neonatal HSV

rows: Onset day 10–12; grouped vesicles at trauma sites; conjunctivitis; no visceral/CNS
Excellent (mortality 0%), but ¾ → CNS/disseminated if untreated
Onset ~day 16–17; ≤70% have skin lesions; seizures; prematurity + seizures at onset = worse prognosis
Sepsis, liver failure, encephalitis, DIC, respiratory distress; 40% NO skin lesions
Present at birth: widespread erosions, bullae, scars, hypo/hyperpigmentation, aplasia cutis + microcephaly, hydranencephaly, calcifications, chorioretinitis

blocks.text: 7.3 SEM Disease — Bedside Details
Vesicles: isolated or grouped on erythematous base → evolve to pustules, crusts, erosions
Sites of vesiculation: presenting part + fetal scalp electrode sites (trauma sites)
If untreated: ¾ progress to disseminated or CNS disease
SEM + ≥3 recurrences of skin vesicles ใน first 6 months → ↑ risk neurologic impairment → indication for suppressive therapy (Kimberlin NEJM 2011)

⚠️ 40% ของ Disseminated HSV ไม่มี Skin Vesicles
ถ้า neonate มี sepsis-like picture, new seizures, liver failure, DIC, respiratory distress — อย่ารอรอยผื่น vesicle ก่อน start acyclovir. Pinninti & Kimberlin Arch Dis Child FN 2014: 40% ของ disseminated disease มี NO cutaneous findings. ถ้า clinical suspicion high → start IV acyclovir ทันที + workup; delay therapy → ↑ mortality markedly.
8. HSV — Diagnosis & Treatment
8.1 Diagnostic Workup
Tzanck smear + direct fluorescent antibody: rapid แต่ variable yield (depends on age/quality/handling of specimen) — ไม่ reliable สำหรับ rule out
Preferred: viral culture + PCR for HSV DNA; CSF PCR sensitivity 75–100%
Specimen sites: CSF, oropharynx, anus, conjunctivae, nasopharynx, peripheral blood; skin scrapings from base of vesicle
Peripheral blood PCR: helps establish diagnosis แต่ ไม่บอก extent of disease
HSV serology: ไม่ useful as diagnostic adjunct
↑ serum ALT → suggests disseminated disease (liver involvement)
CSF ใน CNS disease: repeat PCR at end of therapy; ถ้า positive → continue acyclovir จนกว่า PCR negative
8.2 Treatment — IV Acyclovir
Side effects: transient neutropenia (monitor ANC); nephrotoxicity (↓ risk ถ้า normal renal function + fluid status closely monitored)
Kimberlin NEJM 2011: oral acyclovir suppression × 6 months after neonatal HSV → ↑ neurodevelopmental outcomes ใน survivors of CNS disease
8.3 Prevention of Vertical Transmission
Cesarean delivery ลด risk ถ้า active lesions หรือ prodromal symptoms at time of delivery (Brown JAMA 2003)
Maternal suppressive acyclovir near term: ↓ genital lesions + cesarean rate; unclear if reduces neonatal acquisition
Limit invasive monitoring (fetal scalp electrodes) ใน HSV-shedding mothers
8.4 DDx — Vesicopustular Eruptions in the Newborn (Box 93.1)
9. NICU Practical — Bedside Approach

Vesicular/pustular lesion ใน neonate: ต้อง rule out infectious causes ก่อนเสมอ (แม้ benign). Exam ทุก lesion: distribution, morphology (flaccid vs tense, grouped vs scattered), age at onset, associated signs

Suspected bullous impetigo: Gram stain + culture; start systemic penicillinase-resistant antibiotic IV; isolate; ดู periumbilical lesions → screen for omphalitis; ตรวจเรื่อง MRSA risk ในพื้นที่ → ปรับ antibiotic
SSSS vs TEN: ถ้า clinical suspicious for SSSS แต่ mucosal involvement present → emergency frozen section biopsy; start oxacillin ± clindamycin; monitor fluid/electrolytes/temperature
Omphalitis: erythema + swelling + discharge ที่ umbilical stump → admit + IV ampicillin + gentamicin; Gram stain + culture; monitor for systemic spread (sepsis, NF, peritonitis)

Candida diaper rash ไม่ response ต่อ barrier cream: KOH prep + start topical miconazole/nystatin; ถ้า oral thrush ร่วม → treat both; ถ้า VLBW + CCC-like lesions → ส่ง cultures + empiric systemic antifungal
Suspected IC in VLBW: ส่ง blood + urine + CSF culture; eye exam + echo + abdominal US; start fluconazole/amphotericin B ไม่รอ cultures; อย่าใช้ echinocandin ถ้ายังไม่ rule out eye involvement
Suspected neonatal HSV: ส่ง PCR จาก CSF + oropharynx + anus + conjunctivae + nasopharynx + peripheral blood; start IV acyclovir 60 mg/kg/day ทันที ไม่รอผล PCR; monitor ANC + renal function
SEM disease confirmed: complete 14 days IV acyclovir; discharge พร้อม oral acyclovir suppression × 6 months (Kimberlin NEJM 2011 — ↑ neurodevelopment)
Nursery S. aureus outbreak: ประสาน Infection Control ทันที; isolate cases; active surveillance cultures; staff nose/throat swabs; mupirocin decolonization; ↑ hand hygiene compliance
10. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — SSSS Blister Fluid เป็น Sterile เสมอ
ใน SSSS: S. aureus อยู่ที่ distant focus (nose, mouth, umbilicus, circumcision site) — ไม่ใช่ที่ skin lesion. Blister fluid = sterile เพราะ toxin circulates. ดังนั้น culture จาก blister เป็น negative ไม่ตัด SSSS. Swab จาก nasopharynx/conjunctiva/umbilicus ให้ organism — ส่งไปด้วยเสมอ. Frozen section biopsy = definitive DDx from TEN.

PEARL 2 — 40% ของ Disseminated HSV ไม่มี Skin Lesions
อย่ารอ skin vesicles ก่อน treat HSV. Sepsis-like picture + seizures + liver failure ใน neonate = disseminated HSV จนกว่าจะ prove otherwise. เก็บ PCR ครบ (CSF, oropharynx, anus, conjunctivae, blood) + start acyclovir 60 mg/kg/day IV ทันที. Mortality ถ้า delay = ~30% ใน disseminated disease.

PEARL 3 — Fluconazole Prophylaxis: NICU Rate Matters
Fluconazole prophylaxis ไม่ใช่ standard ทุก NICU. Benjamin JAMA 2014 RCT (VLBW <750g, US/EU NICUs with IC rate <15%): ไม่พบ reduction ใน IC หรือ death. แนะนำเฉพาะ NICUs ที่มี IC rate ≥15%. เช็ค local IC rate ก่อน implement prophylaxis — blanket use = ↑ risk resistance + cost.

PEARL 4 — Echinocandin: ห้ามใช้ถ้ามี Eye Involvement
Echinocandins (caspofungin) ไม่ penetrate vitreous → inappropriate ถ้า IC มี ophthalmologic involvement (Candida endophthalmitis). ตรวจ ophthalmologic exam ก่อน เสมอ ก่อนเลือก antifungal. ถ้า eye involvement → fluconazole หรือ amphotericin B (ยกเว้น liposomal ถ้า urinary tract involved). Treat ≥21 days หลัง microbiologic clearance.

PEARL 5 — Clindamycin ใน SSSS: Toxin Inhibition > Bactericidal
Clindamycin advantage ใน SSSS ไม่ใช่แค่ bactericidal activity แต่เพราะ inhibits exfoliative toxin production ใน S. aureus (ribosome-level inhibition ของ protein synthesis). Wang et al. 2020: clindamycin + oxacillin combo จนกว่าจะได้ sensitivities = best practice. แต่ clindamycin-resistant strains พบได้ใน บางพื้นที่ — ต้องรู้ local antibiogram.
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Ericson JE, Popoola VO, Smith PB et al. JAMA Pediatr 2015;169:1105: Retrospective 348 NICUs: invasive S. aureus infection 44.8/10,000 infants; mostly MSSA. Establishes epidemiology of neonatal staphylococcal infections.

Popoola VO et al. Infect Control Hosp Epidemiol 2016;37:381: Active surveillance cultures + decolonization (mupirocin + chlorhexidine baths) reduced S. aureus infections in NICU — evidence for surveillance-based intervention.
Braunstein I, Wanat KA et al. Pediatr Dermatol 2014;31:305: Antibiotic sensitivity in pediatric SSSS: regional variability in clindamycin resistance; highlights need for local antibiogram guidance.
Wang Z, Feig JL, Mannschreck DB, Cohen BA. 2020;37:222: SSSS antibiotic sensitivity and clinical outcomes: empiric oxacillin + clindamycin recommended until sensitivities return.

Stewart D, Benitz W. Pediatrics 2016;138(3):e20162149: AAP Clinical Report — umbilical cord care: dry cord care for hospital births; topical chlorhexidine for home/low-resource setting with high neonatal mortality. Defines current standard.

Benjamin DK Jr, Stoll BJ, Gantz MG et al. Pediatrics 2010;126:e865: Neonatal candidiasis epidemiology in NICUs: risk factors validated; VLBW, catheters, 3rd gen cephalosporins, steroids, parenteral nutrition — framework still in use.

Benjamin DK Jr, Hudak ML, Duara S et al. JAMA 2014;311:1742: RCT fluconazole prophylaxis in VLBW <750g in US/EU NICUs (IC rate <15%): no reduction in IC or death — universal prophylaxis not appropriate for low-rate NICUs.

Kaufman DA, Coggins SA, Zanelli SA, Weitkamp JH. Clin Infect Dis 2017;64:1387: Congenital cutaneous candidiasis: prompt systemic treatment associated with improved outcomes in neonates — systemic rx for all at-risk (VLBW/preterm).
Darmstadt GL, Dinulos JG, Miller Z. Pediatrics 2000;105:438: CCC clinical presentation, pathogenesis, and management guidelines — landmark review; established CCC treatment framework.

Blanco D, van Rossem K. Pediatr Dermatol 2013;30:717: 2-year prospective assessment: 0.25% miconazole nitrate × 7 days → ~50% cure rate for diaper candidiasis; repeated treatment did not induce antifungal resistance.

Kimberlin DW, Lin CY, Jacobs RF et al. Pediatrics 2001;108:230: Safety and efficacy of high-dose IV acyclovir (60 mg/kg/day) for neonatal HSV — established current dosing standard; favorable safety profile with monitoring.

Kimberlin DW, Whitley RJ, Wan W et al. N Engl J Med 2011;365:1284: Oral acyclovir suppression × 6 months after neonatal HSV CNS disease: improved neurodevelopmental outcomes — now standard recommendation post-treatment.
Pinninti SG, Kimberlin DW. Semin Perinatol 2018;42:168: Comprehensive review neonatal HSV: SEM 45% / CNS 30% / disseminated 25% breakdown; management updates; importance of PCR-based diagnosis.

Brown ZA, Wald A, Morrow RA et al. JAMA 2003;289:203: Cesarean delivery reduces vertical HSV transmission when active lesions present near delivery — foundation for current obstetric management guideline.

James SH, Kimberlin DW. Infect Dis Clin North Am 2015;29:391: Neonatal HSV review: 80% transmitting mothers have no known history of genital herpes — high index of suspicion required in all sick neonates.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: แยก bullous impetigo จาก SSSS: 4 ข้อที่สำคัญที่สุด?

ตอบ: (1) Toxin location: bullous impetigo = local ETA/ETB production; SSSS = circulating ETB จาก distant focus; (2) Blister culture: bullous impetigo = S. aureus ใน blister; SSSS = sterile (S. aureus ที่ nose/mouth/umbilicus); (3) Extent: bullous impetigo = localized; SSSS = generalized + systemic symptoms (fever, lethargy, tenderness); (4) Biopsy: SSSS = intraepidermal split; TEN (DDx) = full-thickness necrosis.
Q: Neonate อายุ 5 วัน มีผื่น erythema ทั่วตัว บวม รอบปาก moist crust ไม่มีผื่นในปาก Nikolsky sign positive — diagnosis? investigation? treatment?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: SSSS (mucosal sparing + Nikolsky sign + generalized erythema + perioral crusting). Investigation: swab จาก nasopharynx/conjunctiva/umbilicus; Gram stain + culture; blood culture; ถ้า diagnosis uncertain → frozen section biopsy (intraepidermal blister confirms SSSS). Treatment: empiric oxacillin + clindamycin IV; fluid support; thermoregulation; bland emollient ใน denuded areas; monitor ANC ถ้า use clindamycin.
Q: SSSS vs TEN: แยกด้วย clinical features อย่างไร? เมื่อไหร่ต้องทำ biopsy?

ตอบ: SSSS: mucosal ABSENT, intraepidermal split, S. aureus-mediated, heals without scar. TEN: mucosal PRESENT (hemorrhagic crusting), full-thickness necrosis, drug-induced, permanent scarring. Biopsy indication: ถ้า clinical picture ไม่ชัดเจน (e.g., mucosal involvement unclear, atypical distribution, drug exposure) → emergency frozen section; ผล real-time: intraepidermal split = SSSS → start oxacillin; dermal-epidermal split = TEN → stop drugs, supportive, dermatology consult.
Q: Omphalitis: organisms, treatment, cord care ที่แตกต่างกันตาม setting?

ตอบ: Organisms: S. aureus, S. epidermidis, Streptococcus spp., E. coli, Clostridium, Klebsiella, Pseudomonas. Empiric treatment: ampicillin + gentamicin IV; switch to oral เมื่อ clinically improved; guide by culture. Cord care: hospital/high-resource: dry cord care (open to air); home birth/low-resource/high neonatal mortality: topical chlorhexidine (Stewart & Benitz Pediatrics 2016). ถ้า extend → NF/sepsis → surgical consult + broader antibiotics.
Q: แยก CCC จาก IC: timing, presentation, treatment approach?

ตอบ: CCC: ascending intrauterine infection; present ≤6 days (usually day 1); skin lesions ที่ back/extensor/intertriginous areas; face/diaper SPARED; palmar-plantar pustules + nail changes; full-term well infant → conservative; VLBW → systemic antifungal ≥14 days. IC: postnatal acquisition; presents >1 week; blood/urine/CSF positive; mortality 20–30%; treat: fluconazole or amphotericin B ≥21 days after microbiologic clearance; NO echinocandin ถ้า eye involved.
Q: ทำไม fluconazole prophylaxis ไม่แนะนำสำหรับทุก NICU? หลักฐานจาก trial ใด?

ตอบ: Benjamin et al. JAMA 2014 (RCT): VLBW infants <750g ใน US/EU NICUs (IC rate <15%) → fluconazole prophylaxis ไม่ลด IC หรือ mortality. Prophylaxis แนะนำเฉพาะ NICUs ที่มี IC rate ≥15%. Concerns: fluconazole resistance (C. krusei, C. glabrata naturally resistant; ↑ use → ↑ selection pressure), ผล drug interactions (e.g., with corticosteroids → QT prolongation). ดังนั้น ตรวจ local IC rate ก่อน decide prophylaxis strategy.
Q: Neonatal HSV classification: SEM vs CNS vs Disseminated — prognosis และ management แตกต่างอย่างไร?

ตอบ: SEM (45%): vesicles ที่ skin/eye/mouth; mortality 0% ถ้า treat; ¾ → CNS/disseminated ถ้า untreated; 14 days IV acyclovir + oral suppression × 6 months. CNS (30%): 70% มี skin lesions; ↑ mortality + neurodevelopmental sequelae; prematurity + seizures at onset = poor prognosis; 21 days IV acyclovir + repeat CSF PCR. Disseminated (25%): 30% mortality; 40% NO skin lesions; 20% survivors neuro delays; 21 days IV acyclovir; monitor liver + coagulation + respiratory.
Q: Oral acyclovir suppression หลัง neonatal HSV — evidence และ indication?

ตอบ: Kimberlin NEJM 2011: oral acyclovir suppression 300 mg/m²/dose TID × 6 months หลัง neonatal HSV CNS disease → improved neurodevelopmental outcomes at 12 months. Indication: all types of neonatal HSV (SEM, CNS, disseminated) after completion of IV therapy. Additional indication for SEM: ≥3 skin recurrences ใน first 6 months → ↑ neurologic impairment risk → suppression เป็น preventive strategy.
Q: Vesicopustular rash ใน neonate: DDx ครบ 8 รายการ? clue สำหรับแยกแต่ละ diagnosis?

ตอบ: Box 93.1 DDx: (1) Transient neonatal pustular melanosis: sterile, resolves spontaneously, more in dark-skinned infants; (2) Erythema toxicum: benign, eosinophils on smear; (3) Staphylococcal impetigo: gram + cocci in clusters; (4) Congenital candidiasis: KOH → pseudohyphae + budding yeasts, nail changes; (5) Herpes virus: grouped vesicles at trauma sites, PCR; (6) Listeria: maternal febrile illness; (7) Syphilis: RPR/VDRL, bone changes; (8) Langerhans cell histiocytosis: CD1a+ Birbeck granules on biopsy, systemic features (bone, lung, liver). ล้วนต้องแยกด้วย history + morphology + labs + culture/PCR.
Day 94 Preview

Avery Chapter 94 — Common Newborn Dermatoses
พรุ่งนี้: Common Newborn Dermatoses — จาก infectious skin conditions (Ch93) ย้ายไป non-infectious rashes ที่พบบ่อยใน nursery/NICU. เนื้อหาหลัก: erythema toxicum neonatorum, transient neonatal pustular melanosis, miliaria, seborrhea, neonatal acne, diaper dermatitis (non-Candida types) — ล้วน benign แต่ต้องแยกจาก infectious causes ใน Ch93 ให้ได้. NICU relevance: ป้องกัน unnecessary antibiotic exposure + workup ที่ invasive โดยไม่จำเป็น.
